title: LOW INCOME COMMUNITY SOLAR - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT LOW INCOME COMMUNITY SOLAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers monthly electric bill credits with a discounted subscription to the Oregon Community Solar Program. Designed to expand solar access and lower utility costs. The subscription to a solar "farm" allows access to solar energy for people who don't have the physical space or financial resources for a solar installation, people who live in condos or apartments, and people who rent or move around a lot. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Residential customers of Portland General Electric, Pacific Power, or Idaho Power with an active account in their name and service address where they live. Must meet income requirements. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (971) 544-8718 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 284-6827 Main phone Main phone (971) 544-8718 Español (971) 544-8720 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call and leave voicemail -Request an appointment online or email to schedule one -Visit website for more information -No in-person enrollment FEES: Discounted monthly subscription fee. Full subscription fee cost is covered by monthly earned solar bill credits. DOCUMENTS: Copy of recent electric bill. No proof of income or ID required. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 20 stops at 28th and E Burnside Street Services: Discounted Electric Service * Low Income Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - DIY SUMMER WEATHERIZATION WORKSHOPS HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT
